agentRecommendation.reasoning: All three candidates have clean CC-BY 3.0 licences — no SA override needed! Candidate A (portrait, 3240x4320) is the strongest editorial image but requires centre-crop to 16:9 that loses ~40% of the frame. Candidate B (landscape, 4320x3240) is natively landscape and avoids the crop penalty. The Pinot Noir bunches composition reads as 'wine region' immediately. Editorially it's a specimen study, not a sweeping landscape — but it's Peninsula-verifiable and licence-clean.
agentRecommendation.alternativeRecommendation: Candidate A with centre-crop if editor prefers the vine-row energy over the bunch close-up. Or defer to Visit Victoria Content Hub for a wide cellar-door-at-dawn frame.

rejected.note: Portrait orientation needs aggressive 16:9 crop. Strong image but loses too much in reformatting.
Chardonnay version — Pinot Noir is the more iconic Peninsula varietal for the hub hero.

notes: AWAITING EDITOR SIGNATURE. Clean CC-BY 3.0 licence — no SA override needed. This is one of only two Tier A hub slots with a clean-licence candidate ready to go.
